name: Salsa Social Dance

interaction_intensity.label: Fast-Paced Socializing
interaction_intensity.description: High energy and fast footwork. Socializing happens rapidly between 4-minute songs.

seasonal_context.peak_note: Live Band nights carry a higher cover charge but offer the best 'Vibe-to-Cost' value.
seasonal_context.off_peak_note: DJ-only nights are cheaper and better for technical practice.

budget_tips: Avoid ordering glass-bottled drinks; they are often restricted on the dance floor, leading to 'lost' half-full drinks.
Stick to 'Aguardiente' shots for a culturally authentic, lower-cost kick.
social_dynamics: Extremely social; frequent partner swapping; visible 'levels' of expertise on the floor.
solo_tips: Nod or smile at the DJs; they often know who the friendly regulars are.
If you're a 'follow,' keep your hand visible to be asked to dance.

safety_factors.label: Moderate Risk
safety_factors.description: Risk of being stepped on or elbowed during fast turns in a crowded room.

variants.name: Singles-Only Salsa Mixer
variants.occurrence_note: A dedicated event where relationship status is the primary entry filter.

variants.name: Pre-Social Rotation Lesson
variants.occurrence_note: The first 60 minutes of the night; partner rotation is enforced by the instructor.

physical_proximity: High; constant movement and spinning in a defined 'slot'.
recommendations: Watch your elbows in tight spaces

name: Bachata Social

interaction_intensity.label: Physical Connection
interaction_intensity.description: Requires close physical proximity with partners. High focus on 'the lead' and 'the follow'.

seasonal_context.peak_note: Congress/Festival weekends see higher entry fees and 'Guest DJ' surcharges.
seasonal_context.off_peak_note: Weekly weeknight socials are the most cost-effective way to practice.

budget_tips: Eat a full meal before arriving; dance socials rarely have affordable or healthy food options.
Check for 'Student' or 'Member' discounts if you attend the pre-social lesson.
social_dynamics: Rotational partner dancing; brief but intimate interactions; technical skill-sharing.
solo_tips: Attend the 'pre-social' lesson to meet people before the floor gets crowded.
Standing near the edge of the floor is the universal signal that you are looking for a dance.

safety_factors.label: Low Risk
safety_factors.description: Generally respectful community, though close physical contact requires clear personal boundaries.

variants.name: Singles-Only Mixer
variants.occurrence_note: Commonly hosted once a month as a dedicated event.

variants.name: Pre-Social Rotation Lesson
variants.occurrence_note: Happens 1 hour before the main social starts.

physical_proximity: Very high; chest-to-chest or hand-to-hand contact is standard.
recommendations: Wear shoes with suede or smooth soles

name: Bar Crawls

interaction_intensity.label: Moderate-High Pressure
interaction_intensity.description: High momentum; the expectation to move with the group can feel pressured if you prefer a slower pace.

seasonal_context.peak_note: Holiday crawls (St. Paddy's, Halloween) see 2x drink prices and mandatory cover charges.
seasonal_context.off_peak_note: Local-run weekday crawls are often subsidized by bars to drive traffic.

budget_tips: The ticket covers entry, but 'batch shots' are usually overpriced; stick to the 'standard' beers included in the route.
Pre-calculate your 'Cash-Only' limit to avoid high-fee ATMs during the crawl.
social_dynamics: Moving group energy, shared mission, collective decision-making.
solo_tips: Introduce yourself to the organizer immediately; they are your safety anchor.
Keep a digital copy of the route so you don't have to rely on following the crowd.

safety_factors.label: Moderate-High Risk
safety_factors.description: Moderate-high due to rapid venue transitions and the likelihood of getting separated from the main group.

physical_proximity: Frequent contact during transitions and while moving through narrow doors.
recommendations: Set a meeting point with a friend if you get separated
Carry a portable charger for long routes

name: Kizomba Social

interaction_intensity.label: High Intimacy
interaction_intensity.description: Known for a 'grounded' and very close connection. Can feel intense for solo newcomers.

seasonal_context.peak_note: International workshops bring high-tier instructors and higher 'Full Pass' costs.
seasonal_context.off_peak_note: Intimate local 'Practicas' are often half the price of a full social.

budget_tips: The 'intimacy' of the dance means you'll spend more time on the floor than at the bar—budget for high-quality hydration over alcohol.
Reusable bottles are often permitted here; check house rules to save on water costs.
social_dynamics: Slow, rhythmic, and highly focused on the partner connection over 'flashy' moves.
solo_tips: Closing your eyes can help you focus on the rhythm if you feel self-conscious solo.
It’s okay to say no to a dance if the 'vibe' doesn't feel safe.

safety_factors.label: Close Contact Risk
safety_factors.description: Low physical danger, but the level of intimacy requires high comfort with personal space.

recommendations: Focus on your breath to stay relaxed during close dances

name: Swing Dance

interaction_intensity.label: Athletic Social
interaction_intensity.description: Bouncy and energetic. Interaction is often lighthearted and less 'intimate' than Latin styles.

seasonal_context.peak_note: Vintage 'Theme Nights' may have higher entry fees due to live big bands.
seasonal_context.off_peak_note: Weekly community dances are usually flat-rate and very affordable.

budget_tips: Craft sodas are a staple here; they are cheaper than beer and fit the vintage aesthetic.
Many swing communities are non-profits; ask about 'volunteer for entry' programs.
social_dynamics: Vintage aesthetic common; very welcoming to beginners; community-centric.
solo_tips: The 'birthday dance' or 'jam circle' is a great time to cheer and feel included without a partner.
Wear vintage-inspired gear to immediately feel like part of the subculture.

safety_factors.label: Physical Risk
safety_factors.description: Kicks and 'aerials' (if allowed) can cause accidental contact.

physical_proximity: Moderate; arms-length to close-hold, but with lots of dynamic movement.
recommendations: Avoid 'aerial' moves unless you have a designated partner

name: Expat & Digital Nomad Meetups

interaction_intensity.label: High Verbal Interaction
interaction_intensity.description: Heavy focus on networking and life-story sharing. Expect to answer 'where are you from?' frequently.

seasonal_context.peak_note: High season for tourism brings 'tourist pricing' to the cafes hosting these events.
seasonal_context.off_peak_note: Low season meetups are often hosted in cheaper, more authentic local spots.

budget_tips: These are often 'Pay for what you consume'—it’s easy to over-order while talking. Keep your tab open to track it.
Choose the 'Local Lager' over imports to blend in and save significant cash.
social_dynamics: Inclusive but transient; high turnover of people; immediate common ground (travel/work).
solo_tips: Wear something that represents your hometown or a recent trip; it’s a magnet for fellow travelers.
Look for the 'organizer' badge if you feel lost.

safety_factors.label: Low Risk
safety_factors.description: Generally safe, public locations; keep an eye on your laptop/tech bag if bringing work gear.

physical_proximity: Moderate; typical of a crowded cafe or hotel lobby.
recommendations: Have a digital business card or QR code ready
Don't feel pressured to stay if the 'career talk' gets too heavy

name: Ecstatic Dance / Movement Jam

interaction_intensity.label: Non-Verbal Social
interaction_intensity.description: Strict 'no-talking' rules on the floor. Interaction is purely through movement and eye contact.

seasonal_context.peak_note: Weekend morning sessions are the most popular and may require pre-booking.
seasonal_context.off_peak_note: Weekday evening 'jams' are often smaller and offer 'sliding scale' pricing.

budget_tips: Since alcohol is banned, your budget is purely for entry and Cacao. One cup is usually enough for the whole 'journey'.
Bring a reusable bottle; filtered water stations are standard in these communities.
social_dynamics: High vulnerability; 'dance like no one is watching'; communal but solitary.
solo_tips: The 'opening circle' is mandatory for the vibe; don't skip it.
If you want to dance alone, simply keep your eyes closed or look at the floor.

safety_factors.label: Low Risk
safety_factors.description: Drug and alcohol-free environments; high emphasis on 'consent' and personal space.

physical_proximity: Variable; can range from wide-open space to intense, swirling crowds.
recommendations: Wear layers—you will get hot, then cool down quickly

name: Potluck / Private Dinner Party

interaction_intensity.label: Intimate Social
interaction_intensity.description: Harder to 'hide' in a small group. Requires active participation in conversation and dining.

seasonal_context.peak_note: Holiday dinners (Nov/Dec) involve higher 'ingredient costs' for your contribution.
seasonal_context.off_peak_note: Summer potlucks are the cheapest; focus on seasonal produce and outdoor settings.

budget_tips: The 'Cost of Entry' is your dish. Bring a high-volume, low-cost item (like pasta or salad) to maximize value.
BYOB means you control your beverage spend entirely.
social_dynamics: Domestic; collaborative; high focus on hospitality and shared contribution.
solo_tips: Volunteer to help with the dishes or serving; it gives you a 'task' if conversation lulls.
Bring a dish that has a story behind it to act as a conversation starter.

safety_factors.label: Personal Risk
safety_factors.description: Higher risk if you don't know the host well; always share the address with a friend before entering a private residence.

physical_proximity: High; shoulder-to-shoulder at a dining table.
recommendations: Always ask the host about allergy-safe zones for your dish

name: Cultural Events & Festivals

interaction_intensity.label: Observational Social
interaction_intensity.description: Low pressure to talk. Most interaction is transactional (buying food) or observational (watching performances).

seasonal_context.peak_note: Peak weekend afternoons see 'Surge Pricing' on food stall items.
seasonal_context.off_peak_note: Sunday evenings (closing time) often see food stalls discounting items to clear stock.

budget_tips: Share 'Small Plates' with a friend to taste more varieties without paying for full meals.
Carry physical cash; many smaller cultural stalls charge extra for credit card processing.
social_dynamics: Family-friendly; community pride; educational and sensory-heavy.
solo_tips: Follow the longest food line—it’s usually the best 'event' of the festival.
Check the stage schedule to time your arrival for the main performance.

safety_factors.label: Environmental Risk
safety_factors.description: High sun exposure, uneven terrain (parks), and large crowds of all ages.

physical_proximity: High in food lines; spacious in viewing areas.
recommendations: Carry cash as some stalls may not take cards
Identify the first-aid tent upon arrival
